global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic outdoor scene of a residential Phoenix neighborhood in extreme summer heat. The composition shows a flat-roofed Southwestern stucco house in the middle distance, with mature saguaro cacti and palo verde trees in the foreground. The sky is a piercing pale blue-white with intense sun glare. A large outdoor mercury thermometer mounted on a wooden post in the foreground dominates the right third of the frame, with the red mercury column maxed out near the top, displaying a clearly readable temperature reading of 115. Heat shimmer rises from the asphalt driveway. Dust hangs in the air. The light is harsh, direct, blinding, with the kind of midday Sonoran-desert glare that flattens shadows. The top 18% of the frame is uncluttered pale sky reserved for headline overlay. The bottom 14% of the frame is empty pavement / dirt foreground reserved for a CTA banner overlay.
global_context.time_of_day: midday, peak summer, June or July afternoon
global_context.weather_atmosphere: blazing hot, dry, sun-bleached Phoenix summer

lighting.color_temp: warm but bleached, almost white in highlights

composition.camera_angle: eye-level, slight upward tilt to catch the thermometer and sky
composition.depth_of_field: deep focus, both foreground thermometer and house in middle distance sharp
composition.notes: top 18% of frame is reserved for headline overlay (clear sky); bottom 14% reserved for red CTA banner overlay (foreground ground)
